Solution

Q8 MSQ Not checked
Technical • COMPUTER SCIENCE & IT • +1 / -0
Not attempted

A certain permutations of integers stored in an array is provided as an input to the produce of quicksort. After one pass of the algorithm, the status of the array is as follows:
8, 7, 10, 12, 19, 14, 20, 22
Which of the following could be the pivot element in array?

Correct answer: A, C, D
Solution

(a, c, d)
Pivot element: All the elements on the left of the pivot are smaller that it and elements on the right are greater than it.
So, 10, 12, 20 and 22 could be the possible pivot elements in array.

Q28 MSQ Not checked
Technical • COMPUTER SCIENCE & IT • +2 / -0
Not attempted

Which of the following is/are correct?

Correct answer: B, C, D
Solution

(b, c, d)
(a) False: A min-heap can provide next largest element, we need to do a linear search through the
heap’s array, it will take Ο(n) time.
(b) True: Find the next smallest element, the predecessor, may require travelling down the height
of the tree, making the running time Ο(n) in worst case.
(c) True: Binary heap to BST conversion take Ο(n log n) time.
(d) True: By heapify method.

Q29 MSQ Not checked
Technical • COMPUTER SCIENCE & IT • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

Consider we constructed binary search tree shown below starting with an empty tree and elements 2, 7, 12, 14, 15, 20, 25, 28, 30 and 40 are come in any order:

 
Which of the following about the order of elements in input sequence can be true?
Correct answer: C, A, B, D
Solution

(c)
We know that ‘a’ comes before ‘b’ iff ‘b’ is child of ‘a’ and ‘b’ comes after ‘a’ iff ‘b’ is child of ‘a’.
Option (a) 7 is not the child of 12. So false
Option (b) 20 is not child of 7. So false
Option (d) 14 is not child of 15. So false
Only option (c) is correct i.e. ‘7’ is child of ‘20’ and ‘12’ is child of ‘7’.

Prim’s always select the edge with minimum edge weight among the available options.
